name: Stenostiridae
description: Fairy Flycatchers are a small family of dainty passerine birds found in sub-Saharan Africa. They feed primarily on insects, which they catch in flight or glean from foliage.

children.name: Elminia
children.description: A genus of small, crested flycatchers with blue plumage found in African forests and woodlands.

children.name: Elminia longicauda
children.description: The African Blue Flycatcher is a small, brightly-colored blue bird with a long tail. It inhabits forest edges and woodlands across west and central Africa, where it sallies for insects.

children.name: Elminia albicauda
children.description: The White-tailed Blue Flycatcher has bright blue upperparts with white outer tail feathers. It is found in miombo woodlands of south-central Africa.

children.name: Elminia nigromitrata
children.description: The Dusky Crested Flycatcher is a dark blue-grey bird with a prominent crest and whitish belly. It inhabits lowland and montane rainforests across central Africa.

children.name: Stenostira xerophila
children.description: A small passerine bird that inhabits dry, rocky forests in eastern Africa. Known for its distinctive calls and agile hunting style.

children.name: Stenostira
children.description: A monotypic genus of small, strikingly patterned flycatcher endemic to southern Africa.

children.name: Stenostira scita
children.description: The Fairy Flycatcher is a tiny, delicate passerine with grey upperparts, white underparts, and a black mask. It inhabits karoo scrub and fynbos in South Africa and Lesotho, gleaning insects from vegetation.

children.name: Elminia albiventris
children.description: A small, crested flycatcher with blue-grey upperparts and a white belly, found in the montane forests of Central and East Africa. It has a prominent crest and a long tail that it frequently cocks upward. It forages actively in the understorey for insects, often joining mixed-species flocks.

children.name: Trochocercus
children.description: A genus of small, crested flycatchers with blue or grey plumage found in African forests.

children.name: Trochocercus nitens
children.description: A small flycatcher with a bright blue head and throat, dark grey upperparts, and white underparts, found in lowland rainforests of West and Central Africa. It has a prominent crest and a long tail. It forages for insects in the forest understorey, often joining mixed-species flocks.

children.name: Trochocercus cyanomelas
children.description: A striking flycatcher with a bright blue mantle and tail, black head, and white underparts, found in forests across eastern and southern Africa. Its long tail is often fanned and cocked upward. It is an active insectivore that gleans and sallies for prey in the understorey and mid-storey of forests.

children.name: Chelidorhynx
children.description: A monotypic genus of small, yellow-bellied flycatcher found in montane forests of Asia.

children.name: Chelidorhynx hypoxanthus
children.description: A tiny, active flycatcher with bright yellow underparts, olive-green upperparts, and a distinctive black-and-white tail pattern. It is found in the montane forests of the Himalayas and Southeast Asia from Nepal to Vietnam. It forages acrobatically for insects in the forest canopy, often hanging upside-down like a tit.
